label: QuickBooks Online
description: Connect QuickBooks Online to read and write accounting data including invoices, expenses, customers, and reports.

form.title: Connect QuickBooks Online
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to access your QuickBooks Online company data via Intuit's OAuth 2.0.

methods.label: Sign in with Intuit (OAuth 2.0)
methods.description: Open a browser window to authorize Anton through Intuit's OAuth 2.0 consent screen. Requires a free Intuit Developer app to supply a Client ID and Client Secret — no passwords stored, revocable from your Intuit account at any time.
methods.how_to:
## Connect QuickBooks Online via OAuth 2.0

You need a free Intuit Developer app to get a Client ID and Client Secret. This takes about 5 minutes.

### Step 1 — Create an Intuit Developer app

1. Go to [Intuit Developer](https://developer.intuit.com/) and sign in (use your QuickBooks account or create a free developer account).
2. Click **Dashboard → Create an app**.
3. Select **QuickBooks Online and Payments** as the platform.
4. Give the app a name like `Anton` and click **Create app**.

### Step 2 — Get your credentials

1. Inside the app, go to **Keys & credentials**.
2. Choose the **Production** tab (or **Sandbox** for testing).
3. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** — paste them into the fields below.

### Step 3 — Add a redirect URI

1. Still in **Keys & credentials**, scroll to **Redirect URIs**.
2. Add `http://localhost` (Anton uses a local redirect to capture the OAuth code).
3. Click **Save**.

### Step 4 — Authorize

1. Fill in the Client ID and Client Secret below and click **Submit**.
2. A browser window opens to Intuit's consent screen.
3. Sign in with your QuickBooks account and select the company you want to connect.
4. Click **Connect**. Anton receives a refresh token and stores it locally.

### Notes

- The refresh token expires after 100 days of inactivity — reconnect if Anton reports an auth error.
- To revoke access, visit [Intuit Account → Connected Apps](https://accounts.intuit.com/app/account-manager/security) and remove Anton.
- Production apps may require Intuit's app review before connecting live company data. Sandbox credentials work immediately for testing.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: From Intuit Developer → your app → Keys & credentials → Client ID.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: From the same Keys & credentials page, paired with your Client ID.

fields.name: environment
fields.label: Environment
fields.description: Use Sandbox for testing with sample data; Production to connect your real QuickBooks company.
